title: Stabilization of pyrolysis bio-oil using in-situ hydrogenation
abstract: Methods are provided for producing an alcohol-containing pyrolysis product. Initially, a hydrocarbon feedstock is pyrolyzed in the presence of a catalyst system, the catalyst system comprising a basic metal oxide catalyst and a hydrogenation metal catalyst. A pyrolysis product is produced that contains at least one alcohol. The basic metal oxide catalyst is comprised of at least one metal from Group 2, Group 3 including Lanthanides and Actinides, or Group 4 of the Periodic Table of Elements, and the supported hydrogenation metal catalyst is comprised of at least one metal from Group 6 or Groups 8-10 of the Periodic Table of Elements.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method of producing an alcohol-containing pyrolysis product, the method comprising: pyrolyzing a hydrocarbon feedstock in the presence of a catalyst system, the catalyst system comprising a basic metal oxide catalyst and a supported hydrogenation metal catalyst to produce a pyrolysis product that contains at least one alcohol, wherein the basic metal oxide catalyst is comprised of at least one metal from Group 2, Group 3 including Lanthanides and Actinides, or Group 4 of the Periodic Table of Elements, and wherein the supported hydrogenation metal catalyst is comprised of at least one metal from Group 6 or Groups 8-10 of the Periodic Table of Elements. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein a hydrogenation metal of the hydrogenation metal catalyst is supported on the basic metal oxide catalyst. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein the catalyst system comprises a mixture of the basic metal oxide catalyst and the supported hydrogenation metal catalyst, the hydrogenation metal catalyst being supported on a refractory oxide support. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein the hydrocarbon feedstock comprises biomass material. 5. The method of claim 1 , wherein the basic metal oxide catalyst is a Group 2 metal oxide and a hydrogenation metal of the hydrogenation metal catalyst is platinum or palladium. 6. The method of claim 1 , wherein a hydrogenation metal of the hydrogenation metal catalyst is one or more of platinum, palladium, nickel, molybdenum, or tungsten. 7. The method of claim 1 , wherein the Group 3 metal comprises at least one of yttrium, cerium, and praseodymium. 8. The method of claim 1 , wherein the Group 2 metal comprises at least one of magnesium or calcium. 9. A method for treating a pyrolysis bio-oil, the method comprising: passing a pyrolysis bio-oil into a reactor comprising a catalyst system, the catalyst system comprising a basic metal oxide catalyst and a supported hydrogenation metal catalyst, to produce a processed pyrolysis bio-oil having reduced quantities of ketones and aldehydes in comparison to the pyrolysis bio-oil. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising producing the pyrolysis bio-oil by pyrolyzing biomass in the presence of a heat transfer material in a pyrolysis reactor. 11. The method of claim 9, wherein a hydrogenation metal of the hydrogenation metal catalyst is supported on the basic metal oxide catalyst. 12. The method of claim 9, wherein the supported hydrogenation metal catalyst is separate from the basic metal oxide catalyst. 13. The method of claim 9, wherein the basic metal oxide catalyst is comprised of at least one metal from Group 2, Group 3 including Lanthanides and Actinides, or Group 4 of the Periodic Table of Elements. 14. The method of claim 9, wherein the hydrogenation metal catalyst is comprised of at least one metal from Group 6 or Groups 8-10 of the Periodic Table of Elements. 15. The method of claim 9, wherein the basic metal oxide catalyst is a Group 2 metal oxide and the hydrogenation metal catalyst is platinum or palladium. 16. The method of claim 9, wherein a hydrogenation metal of the hydrogenation metal catalyst is one or more of platinum, palladium, nickel, molybdenum, or tungsten. 17. The method of claim 13, wherein the Group 3 metal comprises at least one of yttrium, cerium, and praseodymium. 18. The method of claim 13, wherein the Group 2 metal comprises at least one of magnesium or calcium. 19. The method of claim 9, wherein the processed pyrolysis bio-oil further has reduced quantities of unsaturated hydrocarbonaceous compounds in comparison to the pyrolysis bio-oil. 20. The method of claim 9, wherein the processed pyrolysis bio-oil further has reduced quantities of at least one of acetone, acetol, or hydroxyacetaldehyde.
